refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. To design effectively, it is necessary to break it down into sub-functions that cover measurement, control, and optimization of energy usage, which are key to achieving the energy efficiency KPI.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through system-level energy consumption reports, control logs, and optimization algorithm performance metrics over the evaluation period.
sub_frs: FR1: Measure energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control operational parameters to minimize energy per ton.
FR3: Optimize energy efficiency through adaptive strategies.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy and load sensors with data acquisition system.
DP2: Implement control algorithms adjusting vehicle operation parameters.
DP3: Develop adaptive optimization software module for energy efficiency.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Integrate energy and load sensors with data acquisition system; DP2: Implement control algorithms adjusting vehicle operation parameters; DP3: Develop adaptive optimization software module for energy efficiency.

potential_sources: Data dependency between measurement and control modules
Feedback loop between control and optimization
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but designed to minimize unnecessary dependencies by clear data flow and modular separation.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data is available and reliable.
Risk of control algorithm not adapting well to varying conditions.
Optimization software complexity may impact real-time performance.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing optimization separately to reduce complexity and improve maintainability.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical integration of sensors and control algorithms embedded in vehicle control systems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Send energy consumption and operational data to NGHE Solution for monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive operational commands and parameters from NGHE Solution or Teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication for control and data exchange with NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of sensing and control functions; moderate diffusion due to data sharing.
Contains monitoring and optimization logic; higher diffusion due to cross-module data aggregation.
Limited containment; mainly remote control interface.
Minimal involvement in energy efficiency functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy measurement and control are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize latency and ensure tight integration with physical systems.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle software may increase maintenance burden.
Cross-module data sharing may introduce synchronization challenges.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting optimization logic into NGHE Solution module to reduce Vehicle software complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well-defined and mapped to clear, feasible DPs that address measurement, control, and optimization aspects.

diffusion_risks: Data sharing between Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules may cause interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Vehicle as primary owner of sensing and control; optimization may be reconsidered for NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and control logs accessible through defined interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to Vehicle with interfaces to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Consider modularizing optimization logic separately to reduce Vehicle software complexity.
Clarify interface protocols for data exchange to mitigate diffusion risks.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, the complexity and diffusion risks around optimization logic suggest revising the DP and IM allocation to improve modularity and maintainability.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Measure energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control operational parameters to minimize energy per ton.
FR3: Optimize energy efficiency through adaptive strategies.

revise.why: To separate optimization logic from Vehicle module and assign it to NGHE Solution module, reducing diffusion and complexity.

assumptions: Sensor data is accurate and timely.
Vehicle control systems can support embedded control algorithms.
open_risks: Synchronization and latency in data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Complexity of optimization algorithms impacting real-time control.
evidence_needed: Performance data of embedded control algorithms in Vehicle.
Interface latency and reliability metrics between modules.
